input.command:
git add config/trust-firewall.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: expand trust_circle bash patterns for real-world workflows

The original bash_pattern only covered basic read commands (cat, ls, grep).
Real CC sessions use gh api, git log, cargo test, jq, base64, piped
commands, and many more safe operations that were falling to default ask.

New rules:
- Git read-only ops (log, diff, status, blame, show, branch, etc.)
- GitHub/GitLab CLI reads (gh api, gh pr list, glab api, etc.)
- Build tools (cargo, npm, pip, node, rustc)
- Safe piped commands (cat/curl/gh api piped to processors)
- Common UNIX utilities (jq, base64, sort, cut, awk, sed, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

reason: No matching rule — falling back to default
